query: How do I implement an Agent in Mastra that can call a tool and maintain conversation state across turns?

I’m looking at Mastra workflows and I’m not sure when I should use a workflow vs just chaining a few agent/tool calls together. What’s the difference, and which one is better for building a multi-step automation?
response:
Here’s the simple rule:

• Use an **agent** when you want the LLM to *reason* its way through a task, decide which tools to call, and solve an open‑ended problem.  
• Use a **workflow** when your process has *fixed, deterministic steps* that must run in a specific order, with typed inputs/outputs, branching, or long‑running behavior.

Short version:  
If you know the steps → workflow.  
If the model should decide the steps → agent.

What workflows give you that agents do not:

• Deterministic execution  
• Type‑safe input/output per step  
• Explicit data flow  
• Branching, loops, parallel work  
• Suspend/resume, time travel, snapshots  
• Strong observability for each step

What agents give you:

• LLM reasoning  
• Automatic tool selection  
• Multi‑turn internal decision-making  
• Great for ambiguous or creative tasks

Best choice for multi‑step automation  
If the steps are pre‑defined (e.g., “extract → transform → generate → save”), a **workflow** is almost always the better choice. You can still call agents *inside* workflow steps when you need LLM reasoning.

Helpful Links  
Documentation  
• Workflows Overview — how workflows work and when to use them  
https://mastra.ai/docs/workflows/overview  
• Agents Overview — capabilities and reasoning behavior  
https://mastra.ai/docs/agents/overview  

Examples  
• Workflow with Inline Steps — simple example showing multi‑step automation  
https://github.com/mastra-ai/mastra/tree/main/examples/workflow-with-inline-steps

query: Does Mastra have a built‑in “cache” for an agent’s past chats, like a session store? I’m trying to understand how the memory feature works—does it automatically save conversation history and reload it on the next request, or do I have to wire up the cache manually?

I’m using Mastra Tools with an MCP server and I want to configure which tools get exposed to my agent. How do I define an allowlist/denylist of MCP tools, and can I override a tool’s name/description (or disable it) when registering the MCP client?

I’m trying to add voice to my Mastra app—like letting users talk and having the agent speak back. What do I actually need to set up to make that work, and is there a basic example of how to wire it together?
